summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting on December 5, 2023 at 6 PM. Major items included approving a switch in healthcare providers from Medical Mutual to Anthem (with a 6% increase vs. the 10% renewal offered by Medical Mutual), approving payment for an indigent burial, declaring surplus firearms for sale, appointing a new police officer, approving a disciplinary agreement with the police association, renewing the Police Chief/Township Administrator employment agreement, and approving 2024 non-union wages.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for November 28 through December 5, 2023
Approved switching medical insurance to Anthem, dental to Anthem, and accident/critical illness to Standard for 2024
Approved increase in cemetery fund appropriations of $1,806 from cemetery fund (not ARPA) for payment to Advantage Cremation Care of Greater Cincinnati for indigent burial
Approved declaring five forfeited firearms as surplus and selling them on GovDeals to an FFL dealer
Appointed Maggie Sykes as full-time police officer contingent upon successful pre-employment screening
Approved disciplinary agreement with Goshen Township Police Association
Approved employment agreement with Bob Rose to continue as Police Chief and Township Administrator in 2024
Adjourned the regular meeting at 7:10 PM
action_items: Chief Rose / HR to implement healthcare provider switch to Anthem for medical and dental, and Standard for accident/critical illness for 2024
Chief Rose to list five forfeited firearms on GovDeals for sale to FFL dealer
Complete pre-employment screening process for Maggie Sykes for full-time police officer appointment
Fiscal Officer to process $1,806 payment to Advantage Cremation Care from cemetery fund appropriations
key_amounts: $1,806 for indigent burial payment to Advantage Cremation Care of Greater Cincinnati
Medical Mutual offered 10% increase on healthcare renewal
Anthem proposed at approximately 6% increase over current healthcare costs (5.2% for base plan)
Approximately $15,000 anticipated unencumbered balance in cemetery fund at year-end
notable_topics: 2024 healthcare benefits renewal and provider switch from Medical Mutual to Anthem
Indigent burial responsibility and cemetery fund appropriations
Surplus and sale of five forfeited firearms
Tree lighting community event recap benefiting CP AAA and police department
Local Government Fund and property tax reevaluation legislation support letters
Appointment of new full-time police officer Maggie Sykes
Disciplinary agreement with Goshen Township Police Association
Employment agreement renewal for Bob Rose as Police Chief and Township Administrator for 2024
Tornado flag recovered from Mark Park displayed in hallway
attendees: Chief Bob Rose (Police Chief and Township Administrator)
Maggie Sykes (newly appointed police officer, not present for discussion)
notes: An Executive Session was held to consider a disciplinary matter. The tornado flag recovered from Mark Park was displayed in the hallway, a memento from a prior tornado event. Chief Rose corrected a typo in the agenda noting FFA should have been FFL (Federal Firearms License). There was brief debate over whether to fund the indigent burial from ARPA funds or the cemetery fund, with the board ultimately choosing the cemetery fund. Portions of the transcript appear corrupted with repeated headers and garbled text.
title: BOT 12-5-23
